item.description:
Muesli 0.8

Muesli 0.8 turns your local voice history into a private workspace for understanding, cleaning up, and continuing your work.

What's new

  • Private, on-device Insights: Explore activity trends, word patterns, meeting totals, and shareable contribution cards without sending your history anywhere.
  • Live meeting captions: Follow meetings as they happen with optional local streaming transcription, then rely on the selected meeting model for the final transcript.
  • Meetings that continue with you: Pause and resume recordings, continue an existing meeting, create linked follow-ups, revisit upcoming meetings, and export notes and audio more flexibly.
  • Smarter dictation cleanup: Use optional ChatGPT-hosted cleanup or downloadable local cleanup models, with a clearer model catalog and direct links from Settings.
  • Dictionary suggestions: Muesli can notice repeated corrections and suggest additions to your personal dictionary. Suggestions remain opt-in and require Accessibility permission.
  • Private iCloud sync: Sync text records with the iPhone companion app, filter Dictations and Meetings by this Mac or iPhone, and recover cleanly if initial sync setup takes too long.
  • A guided tour of 0.8: After updating, choose whether to walk through the new features in their actual locations. Skip at any point or replay it later from What's New in Muesli.
  • More local model options: Try experimental Gemma 4 E2B dictation and cleanup, Indic ASR, and Nemotron 3.5 multilingual streaming. Large model weights remain optional downloads.

Reliability and privacy

  • The first dictation after an update now waits for the selected transcription backend to be ready instead of beginning an unusable recording.
  • Live captions and cloud cleanup remain off until you choose them.
  • Error telemetry remains available, while automatic GitHub issue prompts are off by default.

Install

Download Muesli-0.8.0.dmg, open it, and drag Muesli to Applications. Muesli 0.8 requires Apple silicon and macOS 14.2 or later.

The release is signed with Developer ID, notarized, and stapled by Apple.

Muesli 0.7.1

Muesli now learns from your corrections. When you fix a dictated word in the app you were typing into, Muesli can suggest adding that correction to your personal dictionary, so future dictations handle names, places, acronyms, and domain-specific terms more reliably.

What is new

  • Dictionary suggestions from corrected dictation: After a dictation, Muesli can detect when you corrected the text in-place and suggest adding that word or phrase to your dictionary. Suggestions are opt-in, require Accessibility permission, and can be accepted or ignored from a lightweight prompt or from the Dictionary view.
  • Better dictionary management: Dictionary entries now support tuned matching thresholds, improved phrase handling, and a clearer review surface for pending suggestions.
  • Nested meeting folders: Meetings can now be organized in a folder hierarchy, with recursive counts, stable ordering, and search/navigation improvements.
  • iPhone sync onboarding: Added onboarding handoff for the iPhone sync path and CloudKit-backed device identity work.
  • More transcription options: Added SenseVoice as an optional local transcription backend and updated FluidAudio to 0.15.1 for newer local ASR capabilities.
  • More reliable meeting detection: Meeting prompts now require stronger full-duplex evidence, reducing false positives from unrelated browser or app audio.
  • Media pause fix: Pause-media-during-dictation now preserves media that was already paused before dictation starts.

Release quality

  • Hardened production signing/profile checks for CloudKit/APNs builds.
  • Validated the preprod Sparkle update path from 0.7.0-preprod.1 to 0.7.1-preprod.1.
  • Expanded tests across dictionary suggestions, storage/search, meeting detection, media handling, release signing, and update-flow metadata.
  • Signed, notarized, stapled, and verified by Apple.

Muesli 0.7.0

This release focuses on faster dictation start, more reliable meeting microphone capture, and the completed move to the Muesli HQ release/update infrastructure.

What's New

  • Faster dictation activation: dictation now uses the AudioQueue-backed recorder path by default for both Automatic and explicitly selected microphones, reducing hotkey-to-mic latency.
  • Better microphone routing: Muesli now keeps dictation and meeting capture app-scoped where possible, honors your selected microphone, and avoids holding idle mic resources after dictation.
  • More reliable meeting capture: meetings now choose a safer microphone route before recording starts, with health checks that can warn when the local mic path is silent or degraded.
  • Live meeting transcript view: active meetings can show an incremental Live transcript, and checkpoint recovery can preserve partial transcript text if the app is interrupted.
  • Fewer false meeting prompts: scheduled meeting and browser-audio detection have been tightened to avoid prompts from unrelated audio or stale meeting signals.
  • Update infrastructure migration: Sparkle feeds, release assets, download links, and Homebrew metadata now use the Muesli-HQ organization paths.

Quality and Release Validation

  • Updated Sparkle to 2.9.3.
  • Expanded tests around AudioQueue fallback, route-aware dictation, route-aware meeting mic capture, live transcript recovery, scheduled meeting notifications, VAD boundaries, and update-flow validation.
  • Signed, notarized, stapled, and verified by Apple.

Muesli 0.6.9

This release focuses on meeting transcription reliability, summary backend flexibility, and a cleaner dictionary editing workflow.

What's New

  • More meeting summary backends: Added LM Studio plus custom OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic-compatible summary endpoints, with settings validation and endpoint normalization.
  • Background meeting processing: Stopping a meeting no longer has to block the app while transcription and summarization finish, and completion notifications are handed off more reliably.
  • Better meeting detection: Browser audio by itself no longer creates false meeting candidates; meeting detection now requires stronger input activity evidence.
  • Calendar reliability fixes: The Coming Up section and calendar monitor now start correctly for legacy meeting settings and meeting-detection configurations.
  • Dictionary UI polish: Dictionary rows are more compact, and threshold editing now uses a dedicated popover slider for clearer tuning.
  • Expanded tests: Added coverage for meeting detection, summary backend readiness, notification handoff, dictionary threshold editing, and update-flow policy.

Signed, notarized, and stapled by Apple.
